sutta: this is quite a good interesting and important suta once when the venable megia was his attendant the exalted 1 stayed at chalika on the hill there now the approach the exalted 1 saluted him stood at 1 side so standing he said the exalted 1 lord i would like to visit jantu kama for arms do now may as you think timely so the venable media dressing himself before noon taking bowl and row and gamma for arms and when he had wandered about for arms and returned there from and finished his meal he came to the bank of the kimi kala river now while stretching his legs and to and fro along the riverbank the vulnerable megia saw a mango growth pleasing and beautiful and at the site he thought truly this mango grove is pleasing and beautiful proper indeed is it to strive here for a clansman wanting to strive if the exalted 1 would permit me i will come and strive in this mango growth and the venable megia returned to the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and told him all that had taken place and if the exalted 1 permits me i will go to that mango grove and strife and the buddha said stay awhile megia appear for we are alone the second time the vulnerable media spoke saying lord for the exalted 1 there is nothing further to be done there is no adding to his accomplishments but for me lord there is more to be done to what is done there is more to be added if the exalted 1 permit me i will go to that mango grove and strive stay awhile megia still till some other monk appear for we are alone a third time the venable megia spoke saying lord for the exalted 1 there is nothing further to be done there is no adding to its accomplishments but for me lord there is more to be done to what is done there is more to be added if the exalted 1 permit me i will go to the mango grove and strive and the buddha said what can we say to you meghia when you repeat i would strive do now megia as you think fit then the venerable media rose from sitting saluted the exalted 1 and keeping him on his right when do the mango grove and having come he entered the grove and sat down at the foot of a tree to spend the noon day rest and there in that mango grove 3 wicked evil thoughts kept coming to the venerable megia that is to say sensual thoughts malign thoughts or thoughts of ill will and cruel thoughts and the venerable media wondered thus it is amazing and astonishing sir by faith alone have i gone forth from home to this homeless state yet i am still docked by these 3 wicked evil thoughts sensual thoughts malign thoughts cruel thoughts so the venerable magda returned to the exalted 1 and said to him lord while i stayed at mangrove grove 3 wicked evil thoughts kept coming to me sensual thoughts malign thoughts and cruel thoughts and i thought how amazing and astonishing it was that i had gone forth from the home to the homeless life by faith alone for lord i am still docked by these 3 wicked evil thoughts sensual thoughts malign thoughts cruel thoughts and the buddha said for the emancipation of the mind of the unripe or unmatured 5 conditions media are conducive to their ripening or maturing what fry here in megia a monk has a good friend a good companion kalyana mitta a good comrade for the emancipation of the mind of the unripe this is the first condition conducive to the ripening again among is virtuous and dwells restrained by the restraint of the patimoka he is perfect in behavior and resort seeing danger in the smallest form undertaking the training he trains himself accordingly this is the second condition then that thought which is serious and a help to the mind's release that is talk on wanting little on contentment on seclusion on aloofness on strenuous endeavor on virtue on concentration on wisdom on emancipation on the knowledge and vision of emancipation among obtains at will easily and without difficulty this is the third condition then among dwell strenuous in purpose putting away unrighteous conditions taking to righteousness a wholesomeness persevering and energetic he shirks not the burden of righteousness or wholesomeness this is the fourth condition moreover monk has wisdom he is endowed there with as to the way of growth and decay with aryan penetration concerning the way to the utter destruction of dukkha this is the fifth condition conducive to the ripening then the buddha continued these 4 conditions may give virtue beneficial talk strenuous endeavor and wisdom may be expected of a monk who has a good friend a good companion a good comrade then meghia when that monk is established in those 5 conditions 4 more must be made to become by him reflection on unattractiveness to put away passion on meta or good will to put away ill will mindfulness in inhaling acts and exhaling to cut off destruction and the thought of impermanence to uproot the conceit i am for among megya who thinks on impermanence the thought of not self endures thinking on there being no cell he wins to the state wherein the conceit i am has been uprooted to the cool even in this life that's the end of the sutta
commentary: so this is quite an interesting sutra we find that this monk he wanted to go off he was supposed to be the attendant of the buddha that's why he was staying there alone with the buddha and he wanted to take off and go and practice meditation in the mango grove but the buddha advised him not to go saying that they were alone but he insisted on going and after pleading with the buddha 3 times the buddha allowed him to go and as the buddha expected now he went there and he couldn't meditate he was disturbed by sensual thoughts thoughts of ill will or anger and cruel thoughts and because he couldn't meditate now with all these thoughts in his mind he came back to the buddha and generally it is not advisable for a new monk to go and stay alone you can see from here why and in the vinaya the buddha instructed that new monks should stay at least 5 years with their preceptor or teacher who teaches them the dhamma the vinaya how to serve the senior monk practicing obedience humility etc and all this is part of the training there's a lot of things a new monk can learn from a senior monk and when he has all the foundation laid then is good enough for a monk to go off and live alone very few monks can go off and live alone from the start and the buddha gave him this advice that 5 things are necessary for an unripe mind to ripen the first 1 he should have a good friend kalyana mitta this good friend kalyana mitta represents a senior monk a senior manga teacher is called a good friend in the in the dhamma in the sutas a good friend who can guide a new manga along the spiritual path the second quality he should practice the precepts be restrained by the pati moka and the third thing uh the beneficial talk beneficial talk in the sutasa prefers to talk about having few ones being contented on seclusion on aloofness the difference between seclusion and aloofness seclusion means staying in a place which is secluded from lay people generally for example in the forest or in the forest monastery whereas aloofness is even staying in the monastery you try to be aloof from other people not to talk with other persons unless it is necessary there's a difference then strenuous endeavor making effort to strive and then virtue concentration wisdom emancipation or liberation and knowledge and vision of liberation these are the things that are beneficial considered beneficial talk number 4 is a monk should dwell strenuous in purpose that means he strives hard to get rid of unwholesome states and to cultivate wholesome states of mind that's why a person on the spiritual path he should be looking into the mind more than outside the mind when we look into the mind that is the source of all our problems all the ego the mental suffering is all in the mind [Music] and then the fifth 1 the fifth quality here that is necessary here for ripening of the unripe mind is wisdom and wisdom generally we have to listen to the buddha's words to to for a starter and then after that we meditate and then we get no wisdom then after that the buddha said now to obtain the last 4 conditions virtue beneficial talk strenuous endeavor and wisdom [Music] these 4 things may be expected of a monk who has a good friend a good companion a kaliana mita that means if among a new monk dwells with a kaliana a senior monk a good teacher then that teacher manga will teach him to acquire these other 4 qualities virtue beneficial talk or give him beneficial talk and then strenuous endeavor and wisdom and the buddha said 4 other things for other things must be cultivated by among reflection contemplation of the unattractiveness that is the asuba the unattractiveness of the body to put away passion unattractiveness of the body refers to the cultivation of the 32 parts of the body and then the second 1 meta to practice meta to put away anger or ill will amitabh is loving kindness or good will towards others and the third 1 is mindfulness of breathing to cut off distraction this is to stop our thinking because every day we are filled with a lot of thoughts and every time we have thoughts and the ego manifests and the buddha encourages us and not to not to think so we practice [Music] to cut off the thinking and the last contemplation is the contemplation of impermanence to upload the conceit i am this 1 is very important especially the contemplation of the different types of corpses we contemplate that 1 day our this body will die and then after about 3 days you will become a corpse which is bloated blue black smelly and the liquids start coming out from the openings yeah suppose we have 9 openings in the body and all these smelly liquid will start oozing out and then later the after the body swells up early will burst you will open up and then you will be eaten by all the worms and all the maggots if we contemplate the dead dog on the roadside how different stages of decay and 1 day our body will also be like that and then we keep on contemplating like this to upload the conceit that i that i am i exist so these are all very practical advice by the buddha the first 1 to recapitulate the 5 conditions are important the drawing of a monk the first 1 he has to dwell with a good friend a good companion as mentioned in some other if we dwell with a teacher then we learn to respect and to love the teacher to be obedient to that teacher and the second condition is virtue the third 1 is beneficial talk if you dwell with a good monk you can obtain beneficial talk number 4 is you have to strive number 5 have wisdom these are the 5 factors and then after that 4 other contemplations are very useful the contemplation of unattractiveness of the body to put away passion contemplation of matter practice of meta or good will or loving kindness to put away anger or ill will then anapanasati mindfulness of breathing to cut off distraction you know impermanence to uproot the conceit i am the nexuta is also quite a good sit down important 1
